question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the rationale as to why a Veteran's ID card is not an acceptable form of identification when applying for an Access NI check.
answer_text: Thank you for bringing this matter to the Department’s attention.  I can confirm that the HM Armed Forces Veteran card has been added to the list of acceptable documents to be uploaded during the online AccessNI application process.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on the Northern Ireland Civil Service estate strategy review of accommodation needs at County Hall, Coleraine.
To ask the Minister of Health how his Department is assessing the efficiency of the encompass system since it was introduced into the Northern Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: For the first time in history, all HSC Trusts across Northern Ireland are now integrated into one digital system, signifying a major step forward in streamlining patient care.The encompass Benefits Board has developed the strategy for facilitating effective benefits realisation across NI ensuring the benefits plan is delivered and emerging benefits are identified and realised.The first annual benefits yearbook covering the period November 23 to November 24 has been released and shows the qualitative benefits for staff and service users from across Northern Ireland to date. Measurable benefits already being realised include improved outpatient clinic utilisation, saved clinical time, streamlined community documentation and increased safety in the administration of medications.  An array of qualitative measures are also being identified where staff highlight the huge safety and efficiency benefits of a fully integrated platform for delivering joined up health and social care. Experience of other organisations in the UK and abroad who are further ahead in their Epic implementation journeys shows significant improvements across a range of areas including increased first time outpatient appointments, increased inpatient volumes and reduction in length of stay ad DNA’s (Did Not Attend). As the system stabilises and is embedded across HSCNI we would aim to see similar benefits on a regional scale. Used well, encompass will deliver important improvements to care quality and productivity, ensuring staff have access to health information and supporting them to deliver safe and effective care.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline why the self-service check-in system has been removed from GP surgeries.
answer_text: Jayex Technology Ltd, which provided the majority of the electronic Check-in and Patient Call systems, went into administration last year due to financial issues, and is no longer trading.Unfortunately, a new contract was unable to be established with the company (Jayex Ltd) which took over the business of its predecessor. The licensing and support for the Jayex Electronic Check-in devices therefore ceased on 31 March and from 1 April are no longer active.In March 2025 GP practices were informed of the issue. It was explained that the impact on the GP practices was dependent on the type of Jayex product it was using and also which GP clinical system is in use. Practices using the EMIS Web Clinical system in conjunction with the Jayex check in system, have been advised there are plans to reconfigure the devices to work with a new supplier over coming months. Practices will continue to be updated.The Vision clinical system has been withdrawn from the market and all practices using this system will migrate to EMIS when devices will be reconfigured to a new supplier. Practices using the Vision system have been informed of this.Practices using the EMIS Web Clinical system, in combination with the Egton electronic check-in systems or Microtech, will continue to operate as normal.Work is ongoing to minimise the impact to practices.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what feedback has been received from staff since the introduction of the encompass system in the Northern Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: For the first time in history, all HSC Trusts across Northern Ireland are now integrated into one digital system, signifying a major step forward in streamlining patient care.Used well, encompass will deliver important improvements to care quality and productivity, ensuring staff have access to health information and supporting them to deliver safe and effective care. Following their successful Go-Lives, feedback has been received from members of staff in Northern, Belfast & South Eastern Trusts and compiled to create the first annual benefits yearbook covering the period November 23 to November 24.The yearbook shows the qualitative benefits for staff and service users from across Northern Ireland to date. Examples of testimonials from staff in the Northern Trust have reported benefits;of having increased collaboration across the Trust with the introduction of mobile tools like Rover streamlining community documentation and enhancing governance of care regardless of location. the greater collaboration between services have included mental health services which has been beneficial.nursing and midwifery staff in the Trust have noted the introduction of barcode scanners to administer medication has been a great success.the social care team has also seen the benefits of having once system that incorporates health care records allowing hospital-based staff to access to real time patient information, enabling seamless collaboration amongst multidisciplinary teams resulting in more efficient coordinated care. standardised workflows enable staff to develop and implement discharge plans faster, reducing delays in transitioning to community or residential care resulting in timely support for patients whilst also alleviating strain on hospital resources.  the ability to review patient charts from anywhere has expedited patient care and decision-making, the improved communication pathways between acute and community services have led to better resource utilisation across the Trust. Appointment booking processes have also improved, preventing double bookings and ensuring smoother service delivery. Experience of other organisations in the UK and abroad who are further ahead in their Epic implementation journeys shows significant improvements across a range of areas including increased first time outpatient appointments, increased inpatient volumes and reduction in length of stay ad DNA’s (Did Not Attend). As the system stabilises and is embedded across HSCNI following the successful implementations in Southern & Western Trusts, we would aim to see similar benefits on a regional scale.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities if there are any plans to change legislation to allow the Housing Executive to make disability adaptations to the homes of parents who share custody of a child with a disability.
answer_text: The power to make grants for disabled adaptations is contained in the Housing (Northern Ireland) Order 2003. Article 53(2)(b) of the Order requires that the disabled person live in the property as, “his only or main residence...”. This means that for cases where the non-resident parent lives in a private sector home, they are not entitled to a grant as the disabled child will not reside in the property as his “only or main residence”.Officials are currently engaged with the Housing Executive and Department of Health on this complex matter as a part of the wider ongoing review of Housing Executive administered grants. At this stage, there are no immediate plans to change this legislation.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on discussions to secure a pay settlement for further education lecturers.

To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline any discussions they had during their recent visit to the United States of America to maximise the economic benefits of hosting The Open at Royal Portrush Golf Club in 2025.
To ask the Minister of Health to detail the current waiting time for a patient to be seen following a red flag dermatology assessment, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of individuals that have been fined for using an e-scooter on a public road or public space in the last two years, broken down by constituency.
answer_text: Offences relating to the use of electronic scooters on public roads or in public spaces may be prosecuted under the Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1995.  However, there is no offence under this legislation that specifically relates to electronic scooters, and prosecutions datasets held by the Department do not specify the type of vehicle involved, where a prosecution was brought for a relevant offence under this legislation.   Therefore, from information held, it not possible to identify the number of individuals that have been fined for using an e-scooter on a public road or public space, as requested.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline what action she is taking to address His Majesty’s Inspectorate of Constabulary and Fire & Rescue Services’ findings that the PSNI's child internet protection team does not have enough personnel to effectively manage its high workload.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she has considered introducing planning restrictions on converting residential properties into short-term holiday lets.
To ask the Minister of Justice to detail the number of Operation Encompass cases that have not been reported to the relevant school by the 09:00 target since its roll-out.
answer_text: The Domestic Abuse Information-sharing with Schools etc. Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2022, which underpin Operation Encompass, came into operation on 1 April 2022. These regulations were drafted by the Department of Justice in partnership with the Department of Education, the Department for the Economy, and the Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs.While my Department brought forward the regulations and provides funding for the initiative, responsibility for the roll-out and operation of Operation Encompass lies with the Department of Education (DE), the Education Authority (EA), and the Police Service of Northern Ireland (PSNI).The Member may wish to contact the PSNI, Department of Education or the Education Authority directly for further detail on the operational aspects of the scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department routinely carries out checks on claimants, in addition to the online form for reporting benefit fraud.
answer_text: All benefit areas within my Department routinely conduct a range of checks on benefit claims. This work is further supported by my Department’s Benefit Security Division who carry out a range of fraud and error activities. This involves checks on benefit claims.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department conducts surveillance on social media to investigate benefit fraud.
answer_text: My Department does not confirm or deny the existence of any practices used to investigate benefit fraud, which by their very nature should remain covert.Surveillance is governed under the Regulation of Investigatory Powers Act 2000 and includes numerous safeguards including independent oversight, to ensure it is only deployed when strictly necessary and proportionate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, in light of the March 2025 Northern Ireland Audit Office report Health and Social Care Imaging Services, (i) whether he has a programme for upgrading equipment in the Northern Health and Social Care Trust; and (ii) after how many years is equipment deemed to be obsolete.
answer_text: (i) The equipment replacement programme for NHSCT Radiology is part of the regional radiology replacement programme.  Delivery against the programme is subject to available funding through DoH.(ii) The optimum age for replacement is 5 years for US scanners, and 7 years for CT and MRI scanners.  Equipment can continue to be utilised safely beyond these ages, and indeed beyond 10 years of age.  In addition to local Quality Assurance programmes, all equipment is subject to specialist Quality Assurance through the Regional Medical Physics Service.  Definitive obsolescence can be determined through a number of criteria, eg:•	Replacement parts are no longer available;•	Equipment fails robust Quality Assurance testing;•	Image quality is determined to be unsatisfactory.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail the number of Operation Encompass cases that have not been reported to the relevant school by the 09:00 target since its roll-out.
answer_text: This information is not held by my department, it would be for the Minister of Justice to respond in respect of the question raised.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail what is meant by a person trained or experienced in giving social support in relation to the changes to Personal Independence Payment law in April 2016.
answer_text: The Personal Independence Payment (NI) 2016 Regulations defines “social support” as: support from a person trained or experienced in assisting people to engage in social situations.The Department’s interpretation of a “person trained” is those professionally qualified, who have the knowledge and been taught the skills, to assist people who need support to engage in social situations.  “Experienced in assisting people to engage in social situations” is interpreted by the Department as meaning a person, such as a friend or family member, who knows the claimant, and has experience in assisting the claimant, who needs support to engage in social situations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the number of Personal Independence Payment (PIP) claimants have been written to in relation to the change in PIP law in April 2016 around social support.
answer_text: My Department has contacted 19,878 PIP claimants in relation to this change. While most contact has been by letter, some people have been contacted by telephone.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how many homes will be purchased in the East Londonderry area following his announcement that approval has been granted for the Housing Executive to purchase homes for temporary accommodation.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive.The planned acquisition of 600 properties will be focused on reducing NIHE’s current exposure to high-cost temporary accommodation (particularly hotels and B&Bs).While the final locations have yet to be determined the principal considerations will include:Geographical areas where exposure to high-cost temporary accommodation is most prevalent.The nature of the temporary accommodation client groups (single people, couples, families).The availability of suitable properties to purchase that align with the above criteria.Value for money considerations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities, following his announcement on additional funding for Co-Ownership, to detail the measures he will put in place to ensure that the new funding plan supports the construction sector in delivering affordable housing.
answer_text: Co-Ownership does not develop housing; it operates on a “DIYSO” (Do It Yourself Shared Ownership) basis where customers select a property of their choice to purchase through a shared ownership arrangement.The new funding programme will generate around £700m worth of housing activity for a government investment of £153m, contributing significantly to a more sustainable model of financing the overall housing sector, providing a boost to the housing market, construction industry and the overall economy.My officials have worked with Co-Ownership to encourage new partnerships with housing associations and private developers to support the delivery of new build intermediate housing as required by council local development plans. This is a significant move into the new build market for Co-Ownership, it is intended that of the 4,000 homes, 1,200 of these will be new build properties delivered through these partnerships.I have also announced an increase in the Co-Ownership property value limit (PVL) to £210,000 from 1 April 2025, providing applicants with access to a wider range of properties across Northern Ireland, including more new build homes. These actions should provide confidence to the construction sector to continue providing new housing, ultimately adding to overall supply.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of fines imposed on drivers who have fraudulently used a blue badge on their vehicle when the blue badge holder is not present in the last two years.
answer_text: In my recent response to AQW 23337/22-27, I advised the member of the measures my Department use to deter drivers from using disabled parking spaces when the blue badge holder is not present. For clarify, the same measures are used to deter drivers using a blue badge to avail of a parking concession, when the blue badge holder is not present, regardless of the restriction type.43 drivers have received Penalty Charge Notices (PCNs) for fraudulent misuse of a blue badge in the last two years, 2023 and 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any measures in place to prevent drivers using a blue badge to park in a restricted space for the duration of a day, when the blue badge holder is not present.
answer_text: In my recent response to AQW 23337/22-27, I advised the member of the measures my Department use to deter drivers from using disabled parking spaces when the blue badge holder is not present. For clarify, the same measures are used to deter drivers using a blue badge to avail of a parking concession, when the blue badge holder is not present, regardless of the restriction type.43 drivers have received Penalty Charge Notices (PCNs) for fraudulent misuse of a blue badge in the last two years, 2023 and 2024.

To ask the Minister of Education (i) whether the feasibility report to progress the proposed merger of Dunluce School, North Coast Integrated College and Coleraine College has been completed; and (ii) for an update on the progress of the project.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether there are any plans by the Housing Executive to increase the number of properties used for temporary accommodation in the East Londonderry area.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“The Housing Executive records temporary accommodation provision by District Council Area rather than Parliamentary Constituency. The Housing Executive regularly seeks to increase the number of properties used for temporary accommodation across all areas. From 4 April 2024 to 3 March 2025 there has been an increase of 9 units (to 98) within Causeway Coast and Glens District Council and 50 units (to 762) within Derry City and Strabane District Council. Furthermore, the Housing Executive has recently appointed a provider to deliver a West Area High Support Service which will provide an additional 40 units of Supporting People funded accommodation over the coming year. Initial delivery of this service is expected to commence in the next quarter.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any action she is taking to prevent retail crime.
answer_text: Business and retail crime has a real impact on local businesses, staff, individual consumers and the wider community, and a partnership approach is central to preventing and addressing retail crime. The assessment of, and response to any criminal activity is primarily an operational matter for the Chief Constable and policy responsibility for the retail sector lies with the Department for Economy.  Additionally, businesses have a key role to play in partnership with Government, the police and local communities, in tackling crime and building safer communities for all. My Department is fully committed to working with all partners, including businesses, the community and voluntary sector, the Police Service of Northern Ireland (PSNI), and other Departments and organisations to ensure that we have a community where we all feel safe to live and work.  My Department is a member of the Business Crime Partnership (BCP).  The BCP, chaired by the PSNI, consists of representatives from the Department of Justice, Northern Ireland Policing Board, Northern Ireland Retail Consortium, Federation of Small Businesses, Retail NI, Belfast City Centre Management, Trading Standards and Policing and Community Safety Partnerships (PCSPs).  The BCP is a networking, information sharing and engagement forum, set up to share best practice and crime prevention advice to support businesses.  Additionally, my Department, alongside the Northern Ireland Policing Board provides approximately £4.25m annual operational funding to PCSPs to support a range of community safety initiatives.  Each PCSP addresses the issue of business crime within their respective action plans and ongoing initiatives include on-street projects such as Community Safety Warden schemes, radio link and text alert schemes as well as the Business Watch scheme which provides crime prevention advice. Assault of a retail worker can be prosecuted under existing laws designed to protect any member of the public from harm. However, in light of increasing concerns about assaults on a range of workers who serve the public, including retail workers, I also intend to introduce an offence of assaulting a person engaged in providing a public service, performing a public duty, or providing services to the public.  The new offence will carry a higher maximum penalty in the magistrates’ courts than that currently available for the offence of common assault, which applies regardless of the victim’s occupation.  Alongside this new offence, I intend to make assault on such a person in more serious cases a statutory aggravating factor, meaning the courts will be required to reflect that fact in their sentencing decisions.  I intend to bring this new offence forward via my proposed Sentencing Bill which will be introduced to the Assembly by the end of this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 23037/22-27, in relation to cases where there is no record of ownership, whether there are any means for her Department to intervene to ensure that the road is of a reasonable safety standard.
answer_text: My Department is not responsible for maintaining roads which are not within its ownership and will generally only adopt roads that have been brought up to standard either by developers through the Private Streets Determination process or by the owners and/or frontagers in accordance with Article 9 of the Private Streets Order.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any increases in spending she intends to put in place to balance the three campus model of the Ulster University.
answer_text: The current budget process is ongoing.  However, I want to ensure all the campuses at each of our Higher Education Institutions thrive.  I will continue to work with each institution to consider how we can support them in this aim.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) how long the scanner at Causeway Hospital is expected to be out of service; and (ii) the number of patients affected by this problem.
answer_text: (i) The scanner in Causeway Hospital had a fault on Saturday 22 February 2025, and was back in operation on Wednesday 26 February 2025.(ii) 51 patients were affected by the problem, however all the patients affected received appointment dates by 5 March 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the rationale for decreasing the higher education budget allocation from £205.4 million for 2024-25 to £198.8 million for 2025-26, whilst at the same time ring-fencing £7.4 million for the expansion of the Magee Campus.
answer_text: All Departmental budgets are under huge pressure.  Within my Department the Magee Expansion has been prioritised because it is part of my Business Plan and the Executive's Programme for Government.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) any measures in place to stop drivers using disabled parking spaces when the blue badge holder is not present; and (ii) the number of people that have received a fine for this offence in the last three years.
answer_text: All Blue Badge holders are advised of their rights and responsibilities when issued with their badge, which include that the Blue Badge holder must be in the vehicle, as the driver or passenger, and personally availing of the parking concession. Further details can be found at https://www.nidirect.gov.uk/articles/rights-and-responsibilities-blue-badge-holdersAs part of the application process, applicants provide their consent that my Department may contact them to undertake random validity checks of badges displayed on vehicles availing of the parking exemption. Vehicles identified misusing a Blue Badge will be issued with a Penalty Charge Notice (PCN). The holder will also be contacted by my Department to advise them of the misuse and the potential for its removal as a consequence of any further misuse as prescribed in Regulation 8 of the Disabled Persons (Badges for Motor Vehicles) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993.In the last 3 years, 2022, 2023 and 2024, 206 vehicles have been identified for blue badge misuse

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail how long patients who had scans cancelled as a result of the scanner problem at Causeway Hospital are expected to wait for another appointment.
answer_text: The scanner in Causeway had a fault on Saturday 22 February, and was back in operation on Wednesday 26 February. With regards to those that had appointments scheduled for:Sunday 23rd February 2025, the new appointment date was Sunday 2nd March 2025.Those with an appointment date of Monday 24th February and Tuesday 25th February 2025, the new appointment was between 3rd and 5th March.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs, given the importance of the River Bann and its link into Lough Neagh, whether he has any plans to re-establish the Lower Bann Advisory Committee.
answer_text: The Department has no plans to re-establish the Lower Bann Advisory Committee.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any measures that can be implemented by her Department to ensure that an unadopted road meets necessary safety standards for vehicles.
answer_text: Potential public safety risks in unadopted developments are identified as part of the cyclic Private Streets inspection process completed by my officials or notified to my department by the public.Following identification of a defect or safety risk in an unadopted development the department will notify the developer to complete repair works or carry out repairs under Article 22 of the Private Street Order.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether she has given consideration to the impact that a public-private partnership would have on the development of wastewater infrastructure.
answer_text: My Department is aware of the possibilities offered by Public Private Partnership contracts and I am keen to explore all funding options available to increase the level of investment possible in wastewater infrastructure.Public Private Partnerships provide an alternative delivery opportunity; however, funding would still be required to deliver such schemes. Under current public expenditure requirements, the level of investment delivered through public-private partnerships would need to be met from the overall budget. Therefore, such arrangements are limited by the budget available.Furthermore, PPP contracts are long term arrangements, which will require a long-term commitment to fund.Currently my Department has no long term budget certainty, and it is subject to the continuing austerity budget policies imposed on all public services across the North by the British Government.I am committed however, to working with my Executive colleagues, to secure improved levels of funding for our all our public services including for water.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the percentage change in the manufacturing sector’s economic performance over the last three financial years.
answer_text: Question 12 AQO 1596/22-27Manufacturing output in the North increased by a total of 1.1% over the last three financial years (for which data is available). Comparatively, UK manufacturing output as a whole decreased by 1.1% over the year to the third Quarter of 2024. Most recent data indicates that the sector is in growth in this current financial year by 1.3% to the third Quarter of 2024. Invest NI client companies in the Advanced Manufacturing and Engineering sector have grown from 2021 to 2023, overall sales by 26% and export sales by 28%. At the same time employment in the sector has grown by 2.5%.The North was also the only devolved region to see an increase in exports.  In the year to September 2024 total goods exports increased by 5.5% (to £11.2 billion) compared with the previous 12 months. UK goods exports overall fell by 6.3%. exports from England decreased 4.5% and Scotland and Wales down 10.9% and 9.5% respectively.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline what action her Department is taking to ensure the required funding is available to increase the number of police officers to 7,000.

To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs to outline any contact his Department has had with the UK Government to discuss reversing the decision to introduce an inheritance tax on farms worth over £1 million.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether there are plans to deploy traffic attendants to enforce parking restrictions in Castlerock over the summer period to assist with traffic management.
answer_text: Castlerock is not currently included in a weekly enforcement patrol, however, when requested and resource permits, Traffic Attendants (TAs) have visited the town as part of a mobile patrol.Due to the high level of parking compliance, my Department has no plans to add Castlerock to the daily enforcement schedule however, the parking enforcement approach and enforcement schedules are flexible can be adjusted accordingly, subject to the availability of resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail (i) any impact the State Pension increase in April will have on Pension Credit; and (ii) whether current awards of Pension Credit will be reduced accordingly.
answer_text: Pension Credit is a means-tested benefit for people over State Pension age whose income and capital are below a certain level. The basic State Pension is in payment for those who attained pension-age prior to 6 April 2016, and was replaced by the new State Pension after this date.From April 2025, the basic State Pension will increase by £6.95 to £176.45 per week and the full rate of the new State Pension will increase by £9.05 to £230.25 per week. This is a 4.1% increase.Pension Credit rates will also increase by 4.1% in April 2025. The Pension Credit Standard Minimum Guarantee for a single person will increase by £8.95 to £218.15 per week and for couples by £13.65 to £346.60 per week.As with all means-tested benefits, the individual circumstances of each claimant will affect the amount of benefit received. Post-uprated amounts of State Pension will be taken into account in Pension Credit but in most cases will not exceed the higher rate of Pension Credit following uprating.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether there are any plans to introduce grants for householders to install eco-friendly heat pumps to replace old oil heating systems.
answer_text: At present, financial assistance is available through the Northern Ireland Sustainable Energy Programme (NISEP), for fully supported, and part funded schemes, for the installation of energy efficiency measures and heat pumps, with most of the funding targeted at lower income customers.My department has recently consulted on future support of low carbon technologies in residential properties, which closed 10th January 2025. The views provided will support my department in the development of policy to inform options regarding the design, rules, and guidance of future support for low carbon heat, which will include heat pumps.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities in what circumstances does a personal compensation payment affect a person's benefit entitlement.
answer_text: For the purposes of contribution-based benefits, a personal compensation payment has no impact on a person’s entitlement.However, for means-tested benefits, any payments of capital or income may affect a person’s entitlement. The means-tested benefits are:Income Support Income-based Jobseeker’s AllowanceIncome-related Employment and Support AllowanceState Pension CreditUniversal CreditHousing BenefitA personal compensation payment is a payment of capital for the purposes of all means-tested benefits. As such, depending on what it is paid for, and whether a disregard has been included in legislation in respect of a special compensation scheme, a personal compensation payment will be taken into account in the calculation of the award of those benefits.  If specific provision has been made to disregard payments from special compensation schemes, these will normally be disregarded as capital indefinitely for all benefits. For payments that are not from special compensation schemes. If the compensation payment is in respect of personal injury to the claimant or their partner, these payments can be disregarded for the purposes of determining benefit entitlement. In respect of working age benefits, these payments will be disregarded for twelve months from the date the payment was received. After this time, the payment will be taken into account as capital unless it has been placed in trust or used to purchase an annuity. For State Pension Credit, any payment in respect of personal injury will be disregarded indefinitely. If the payment is not paid to compensate for personal injury specifically, then no such disregard would apply and the payment may, depending on the amount, affect a person’s entitlement from date of receipt of same.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether he has considered the reintroduction of the cross-border healthcare directive in order to reduce waiting times.
answer_text: As part of a package of initiatives to reduce waiting list backlogs, the Waiting List Reduction Reimbursement Scheme opened on Monday 2 June 2025.  Subject to prior approval, the Scheme is available to patients, who are ordinarily resident in Northern Ireland, and have been waiting two years or more for hospital treatment on a HSC waiting list, the option to seek and pay for treatment in the private sector and have the cost reimbursed, up to the cost of the treatment to HSC in Northern Ireland.The new Scheme is being initiated on a phased approach.  The first phase will allow reimbursements for treatment in the Republic of Ireland. The second phase will be introduced in the autumn and will see the geographical scope of the Scheme expanded to all countries in the European Union (EU). By opening up the Scheme to the EU, patients will have a greater range of providers to choose from.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health whether a patient can claim any money back following treatment by a private dentist as there was no access to an NHS dentist.
answer_text: There is no facility for the Health Service to provide financial help for patients who receive dental treatment provided under private contract.   Fees charged for private dental care are a contractual matter between the patient and the dentist who provided the treatment.Advice for patients looking to register as a Health Service patient can be found on the SPPG website at: https://online.hscni.net/our-work/dental-services/registering-dental-patient/ Patients experiencing an urgent or pressing dental issue may also be eligible to be seen as part of the Dental Access Scheme.  There are two participating practices close to your constituency with further information on the SPPG website at: https://online.hscni.net/our-work/dental-services/dental-access-scheme/

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has given consideration to legislation in relation to the use of e-scooters in public spaces.
answer_text: The position remains that it is illegal to use e-scooters on public roads and public spaces here. I currently have no plans to change that position. It also remains illegal to use e-scooters on public roads or in public places in Britain unless they are being used as part of the ongoing Department for Transport (DfT) trial of rental e-scooters scheme. This scheme has been extended until May 2026 and DfT may introduce legislation to enable a more general use of e-scooters there once the pilot scheme is complete and its outcomes assessed. You may be aware that legislation was introduced in the South in May 2024 making the use of some e-scooters legal on public roads, subject to specified restrictions. Users must be 16 or older and obey a speed limit of 20km/h. Among other restrictions, e-scooters can’t be used on footpaths or in public places and users can't carry passengers.My officials will continue to monitor the progress of the trial scheme in Britain as well as the impact of the recent legislative changes in the South, and in due course I will consider the way forward here.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities how often the Housing Executive policy in relation to succession is reviewed.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows.“Succession is governed primarily by legislation with some further limited provision for exceptional circumstances through the Rules of the Housing Selection Scheme. The relevant legislation is the 1983 Housing NI Order.The Department led Fundamental Review of Allocations is currently being implemented and the agreed changes to the Housing Selection Scheme Rules around succession were made in September of 2024. These changes were the subject of previous public consultation. There are no current plans to further review Housing Selection Scheme Rules in this area.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Northern Ireland Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with the Northern Ireland Housing Executive will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the rules in relation to NHS dentists providing emergency treatment for patients who have had treatment abroad privately.
answer_text: In line with normal practice, patients who seek medical treatment abroad will continue to be able to access emergency care upon return to the UK where necessary.Patients can access emergency dental services via their registered dentist or at an Emergency Dental Clinic, via the Dental Access Scheme, or at the Urgent Student Clinic at the School of Dentistry.However, where a patient seeks non-emergency pre and/or post treatment care specific to their private treatment, they will not be entitled to such support from primary or secondary care services in Northern Ireland. Therefore, it will be important for the individual to ensure that all pre and post treatment costs are included in the overall package of care procured from the private healthcare provider.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities why Personal Independence Payment renewals are sent out so far in advance of the claimant's award ending, which may result in the applicant's medical conditions changing and information being out of date by the time the assessment is carried out.
answer_text: Personal Independence Payment renewals are issued well in advance of an award of benefit ending to allow adequate time for completion without disrupting payments. This allows my Department to review claims, conduct assessments if needed, and make decisions before the existing award expires. An applicant can, however, provide further information about a change in their medical condition at any point following completion of their award review form.  This will then be considered by the case manager as part of the review process.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any discussions he has had with Ulster University in relation to supporting the Riverside Theatre located at the Coleraine campus.
answer_text: I have not had any discussions with Ulster University in relation to supporting the Riverside Theatre.		The Arts Council is the funding and development body for the arts in Northern Ireland and the Riverside may wish to make  contact via info@artscouncil-ni.org to discuss funding streams which may be available.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any discussions he has had with Ulster University in relation to supporting the Riverside Theatre located at the Coleraine campus.
answer_text: I have not had any discussions with the Vice-Chancellor of Ulster University in relation to supporting the Riverside Theatre.My Department maintains an ongoing dialogue with the University and is open to discussion of any issue affecting the institution and its campuses.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any measures in place to reduce the number of Housing Executive and housing association properties (i) being used as benefit drops; and (ii) being sublet by tenants.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Housing Executive and Registered Housing Associations. In terms of the Housing Executive, I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows.“The Housing Executive prioritises detecting and taking action on tenancy fraud. Local office staff record and investigate all reports of fraud and take action, where appropriate, ensuring housing stock is only occupied by those with the legal right to do so. Key measures in place to prevent and detect non-occupation and sub-letting are:Fraud prevention checks at sign up - Housing Executive staff collect verification documents at sign up, including ID, and take a customer photograph as a mandatory condition of offer acceptance. An unannounced visit must then be carried out to all new tenancies within 2 months of tenancy commencement. The information collated at sign-up is used to ensure the correct tenant is in occupation at this stage and can be used to verify tenant identity on receipt of any subsequent reports of non-occupation/subletting. As part of day-to-day service delivery, staff are required to undertake verification checks on contact with tenants via telephone or at the public counter e.g. if a tenant is reporting a repair via phone the staff member should prompt them to provide their name and details (e.g. DOB). In cases where they are unable to provide this information and sub-letting may be suspected, further investigation can be carried out, if necessary, by front-line staff.Staff are expected to identify any signs indicating possible tenancy fraud e.g. unkept garden, curtains untouched, no accessed annual heating service appointments. Training on fraud identification and investigation has been incorporated into training packages delivered for frontline staff. The Tenancy Fraud Unit is a specialist investigation support team set up within the Housing Executive to assist frontline staff in complex fraud cases such as non-occupation or subletting. This team are trained and empowered to undertake more detailed investigations to evidence suspected fraud. Additionally, the Housing Executive utilise an online reporting facility, whereby informants can report suspected fraud cases online for investigation to the Corporate Investigation Team. The Housing Executive participates in the National Fraud Initiative (NFI), matching electronic data within and between public and private sector bodies to prevent and detect fraud.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the Northern Ireland Housing Executive directly. Making direct contact with the Northern Ireland Housing Executive will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.In terms of Registered Housing Associations, detection of subletting and non-occupation are carried out by staff when maintenance and health and safety checks are carried out. My Department i

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether roads leading to schools in rural areas are prioritised for gritting.
answer_text: The current winter service policy and procedures operated by my Department utilises the widely accepted practice of targeting limited resources for this service on the higher trafficked main through routes and can be viewed on the Department’s website below.https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/winter-service-rsppg-e022The application of this policy means that 28% of the total road network, which carries around 80% of traffic, is salted. It must, however, be emphasised that the service provided cannot eliminate the risks – even on roads that are gritted, ice free conditions cannot be guaranteed.The Department also has priority “secondary” salting to rural schools to ensure they receive a level of special attention throughout the winter period. Currently there are 80 rural schools not on the salted network, on the list for secondary gritting.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the criteria used to decide which rural roads are included in the gritting schedule.
answer_text: The current winter service policy and procedures operated by my Department utilises the widely accepted practice of targeting limited resources for this service on the higher trafficked main through routes and can be viewed on the Departments website below.https://www.infrastructure-ni.gov.uk/publications/winter-service-rsppg-e022The application of this policy means that 28% of the total road network, which carries around 80% of traffic, is salted. It must, however, be emphasised that the service provided cannot eliminate the risks and even on roads that are gritted, ice free conditions cannot be guaranteed.As road users we all need to be mindful of the road conditions and adjust the way we drive, ride or cycle.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on what support his Department is providing to Motor Sport in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Through Sport NI my Department has provided exchequer funding totalling £1.023m for safety improvements in motorsport since 2015/16.Sport NI also awarded £18,000 of lottery funding in 2023/24, and a further £18,000 of lottery funding in 2024/25, to 2&4 Wheel Motorsport Limited, the Umbrella Body for the governing bodies of motorsport, under Sport NI’s Sport Systems Investment – Governing Bodies programme.Sport NI has been working in partnership with 2&4 Wheel Motorsport Limited to identify where interventions aimed at improving safety are needed at events held across Northern Ireland. I hope to make a formal announcement with more detail at the end of this month.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail the support that is available to families with children who end up in temporary accommodation away from their family support.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for the Northern Ireland Housing Executive. I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“Where appropriate, the Housing Executive will seek to secure floating support for households placed in temporary accommodation and staff in Housing Solutions and Support Teams will seek to refer households to projects that deliver housing related support. Housing related support services help people live independently and this can include support to:develop domestic/life skillsdevelop social skills/behaviour managementfind other accommodationestablish social contacts and activitiesgain access to other servicesmanage finance and benefit claims.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact the NIHE directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any plans she has to reduce the number of temporary promotions throughout all Departments in a transparent and time framed way.
answer_text: As Departments are responsible for managing their staffing needs, I can only answer on behalf of the Department of Finance (DoF).Temporary promotions (TPs) are designed primarily to meet business and management needs and can be for several different reasons, examples of which include covering sick absence, maternity leave, special exercises (time bound projects), and to fill vacancies.The DoF People and Resources Sub-Committee regularly provides management information to DoF Departmental Board members regarding the status of TPs to ensure they are regularly reviewed.  This provides an assurance to DoF Board that TPs are managed in line with business need and are satisfied that the process remains fair and equitable.My Department continues to work towards reducing the number of temporary promotions however in instances such as special exercises or short-term projects, filling these posts substantively would create a permanent budget pressure which would remain after the project has ended.Recent budget pressures have resulted in recruitment activity being curtailed while we await clearer financial conditions, which has had an impact on the use of temporary promotions. The majority of TPs in my Department are as a result of Vacant Posts, therefore as they are substantively filled the number of TPs will reduce.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to outline the average duration that civil service staff have been on temporary promotion in the last ten years.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline the location of any Invest NI land in East Londonderry.

To ask the Minister of Health to detail any plans to supply those living with type 1 diabetes with access to insulin pump technology and continuous glucose monitoring systems to better manage their condition.
answer_text: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence TA943, Hybrid closed loop systems for managing blood glucose levels in Type 1 diabetes, has been endorsed as applicable in Northern Ireland. Subsequently, my Department has requested work to be undertaken to understand the implications of this decision.  Overall provision; priority groups; system costs; workforce and communication requirements to both enable and support, are being considered. Given the scale of investment needed, financial requirements are also being considered, as funding is required for devices as well as resources to upskill and recruit the workforce needed to offer Hybrid Closed Loop safely, effectively and equitably across the region.  Final decision-making and commissioning arrangements for NI will be made, supported by an implementation plan. This will support the integration continuous glucose monitoring technologies into diabetes care and will be communicated to all stakeholders.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) the number of Diabetic Specialist Nurses currently employed in each Health and Social Care Trust; and (ii) any plans to increase the number of these nurses.
answer_text: Across both adult & paediatric services, there are currently 19 Diabetes Specialist Nurses employed within the Northern Health and Social Care Trust (15.34 whole time equivalent); 18 in South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust (16.93 whole time equivalent); 18 in Belfast Social Care Trust (16.72 whole time equivalent) and 21 in the Western Health and Social Care Trust (17.28 whole time equivalent).  Information has yet to be provided by the Southern Health and Social Care Trust. A Diabetes Programme of Care Workforce Review is near completion, led by the Department of Health Workforce Policy Directorate.  This review included diabetes specialist nurses as well as other specialist health care professions directly involved in the delivery of diabetes treatment and care. This 10-year forward view will inform workforce planning needs to ensure equitable access to specialist diabetes professionals across Northern Ireland, in order to provide a safe, effective and quality service for people living with diabetes.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail any engagement they have had with the Minister for the Economy on the opportunity to use The Open at Royal Portrush Golf Club in 2025 to promote (i) the East Londonderry constituency; and (ii) Northern Ireland as a place to invest.
answer_text: We are very much aware that The Open is an opportunity to showcase not only our golfing talent, but also our ability to successfully host major events and our tourism potential. As we approach the summer, the Executive will be considering how to maximise the opportunity right across Northern Ireland and these islands.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether his Department has considered issuing a permanent award of Personal Independence Payments or Disability Living Allowance, that is not subject to review, to a claimant who is living with a condition that will not improve.
answer_text: The rules governing Personal Independence Payment provide that it is to be awarded for a fixed term except where the Department considers that a fixed term award would be inappropriate. The rules governing Disability Living Allowance provide that awards may be made either for a fixed period or for an indefinite period.  Personal Independence Payment recipients who have a progressive condition, who have been awarded the highest level of Personal Independence Payment and whose needs are unlikely to change, receive an on-going award with only a ‘light touch’ review after 10 years. This approach also applies to people who are over State Pension Age and in receipt of any rate of Personal Independence Payment.Children in receipt of Disability Living Allowance who have a progressive condition and whose needs are unlikely to change will be awarded the benefit until age 17.  Those over State Pension Age in receipt of Disability Living Allowance will receive an indefinite award, which is only subject to review if there has been a change in the person’s condition.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether the High Street Task Force will work with the new Coleraine Future Town Fund Board to develop a town centre masterplan.
answer_text: We are delighted that Coleraine will benefit from up to £20 million as part of the UK Government Towns Programme over the next ten years. It is a great opportunity for the town.The work of the High Street Taskforce concluded with the presentation of the report to Junior Ministers in March 2022.The scope of the 14 recommendations span the responsibilities of a number of departments. It will be a matter for the Coleraine Future Town Fund Board to engage with the appropriate Departments to take forward any recommendations relevant to their work.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on any proposed farm welfare bill.
answer_text: I can confirm that DAERA will not be taking forward the NI Farm Welfare Bill because there are several factors that make the Bill unfeasible as it currently stands.  Most of Northern Ireland’s agricultural produce is exported for sale and this Bill could adversely impact the international competitiveness of NI agriculture.  Furthermore, anti-competition legislation is a reserved matter and outside of my department’s remit.  I am of course sympathetic to the concept that farmers are adequately rewarded for their work but in a market economy, this must be achieved through the market.  I welcome the introduction of Defra legislation to improve transparency and fairness in different UK farming sectors including the recent introduction of the Fair Dealing Obligations (Milk) Regulations 2024 and the ongoing engagement with other sectors.  My department will continue to work closely with Defra to represent the views of NI farmers in these areas.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when he expects a decision to be made on planning application LA01/2016/1328/F.
answer_text: Planning application LA01/2016/1328/F is currently being processed by Causeway Coast and Glens Borough Council.
